introLines.text: Vern, I've been listening for years and I finally have my own sighting to report.
I never thought I'd be making this call, but here I am.
So I was out stargazing Tuesday night when something appeared that wasn't on any chart.

detailLines.text: Three amber lights in a triangle formation, completely silent, hovering for about two minutes before shooting off.
It moved against the wind, no sound, no blinking lights like a plane would have.
I timed it - ninety seconds of hovering, then acceleration that would flatten any pilot.

defenseLines.text: I've worked at the airport for fifteen years. I know aircraft. This wasn't one.
I'm an amateur astronomer. I know what satellites look like. This was different.
My whole family saw it. We're not all crazy, Vern.

acceptanceLines.text: I knew you'd understand. Nobody else believes me.
Thank god. My wife thinks I've lost it.
That's exactly what I was hoping to hear.

extraDetailLines.text: I managed to get a few photos. They're not great, but you can clearly see the triangle formation.
The way it moved... it was like physics didn't apply. No aircraft I know of can do that.
I've checked flight trackers, satellite databases. Nothing was supposed to be there.

conclusionLines.text: Thanks Vern. I'll send you the photos if I can get them developed.
Keep doing what you're doing. People need to hear this stuff.
I'll keep watching and call back if it happens again.
